summary: Phase 2 Batch 4 (PayPal alongside Stripe) fully green. Created /app/backend/tests/test_phase2_batch4.py with 11 tests covering: integrations catalog PayPal auto_connected, POST /api/payments/paypal/checkout (auth required 401/403, unknown invoice 404, real sandbox order create returning https://www.sandbox.paypal.com/checkoutnow?token=..., audit row paypal_checkout written, invoice.paypal_order_id + payment_link populated), GET /api/payments/paypal/status/{order_id} (created/pending for fresh order; bogus order yields 404/502), and POST /api/webhook/paypal (bogus order accepted with no invoice side-effects; PAYMENT.CAPTURE.COMPLETED flips linked invoice to paid + paid_at; invalid JSON handled). Full regression 105/105 pass (94 prior + 11 new). Frontend smoke via Playwright: login OK, Invoices page renders invoice-paypal-{id} next to invoice-pay-{id} on all 10 non-paid invoices, click on a PayPal button fires POST /api/payments/paypal/checkout → 200 (sandbox navigation aborted in test). Integrations page renders integration-paypal card marked 'connected' and the paypal-webhook-url code block renders the correct /api/webhook/paypal URL.

minor.issue: Signature verification intentionally skipped (PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID unset). Code comment documents this. MOCKED/DEFERRED per review — not a bug, but flip to required before going live.

Requires auth but does not scope by owner — any authenticated user who guesses an order_id created by another owner could read its PayPal status + trigger capture. Consider adding owner_id check against local payment_transactions row before forwarding to PayPal.

Stripe shows 'disconnected' if a prior toggle flipped it, because the existing integrations row overrides the auto_connected default. Consider honouring auto_connected=True unless user explicitly toggled (add 'user_overridden' flag). Low priority.
No single-invoice GET endpoint exists — only list. Fine for now (UI uses list), flagging for later router refactor.

action_items: OPTIONAL: scope /api/payments/paypal/status/{order_id} by owner_id (lookup local payment_transactions row first; 404 if not owned).
OPTIONAL: add PAYPAL_WEBHOOK_ID env + signature verification to /api/webhook/paypal before production.
OPTIONAL: add GET /api/invoices/{id} for single-invoice lookup (UI currently relies on list).
OPTIONAL: server.py is now ~2594 lines — router split still deferred but increasingly overdue.
critical_code_review_comments: paypal_order_status forwards to PayPal using only the order_id from the URL without first checking that a local payment_transactions row exists for (order_id, owner_id=current_user). Cross-tenant read/capture risk if order_ids are guessable.

paypal_create_order formats amount with f'{amount:.2f}' which is correct for PayPal's 2-decimal string requirement; but invoice.grand_total is stored as float — a 49.999 could round inconsistently between PayPal's value and Mongo. Consider using Decimal at the invoice-totals layer.
PayPal webhook signature verification skipped — bogus event can mark transactions paid if an attacker knows an order_id. Sandbox-safe, production-must-fix before go-live.
PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/MODE + PAYPAL_BASE evaluated at import; toggling env without restart is a no-op (expected for FastAPI, but document).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on critical paths (buttons render, API fires, webhook URL rendered, paypal integration card shown as connected)

seed_data_creation: Each run creates 1 TEST-PP-* invoice tied to first-existing contact (falls back to creating TEST_paypal_*@example.com contact). Invoice is left as 'paid' at end of run (real webhook-capture flow). Not auto-cleaned; negligible — they are harmless test invoices, consider adding teardown if count grows. Also creates real PayPal sandbox orders (1 per run) — orders expire automatically on PayPal side after 3 hours.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-6 is 105/105 green. PayPal sandbox integration is live and exercised on every backend test run (outbound to api-m.sandbox.paypal.com). If PayPal sandbox is down, TestPaypalCheckout.test_creates_real_sandbox_order will return 502 and the rest of the PayPal suite will skip cleanly. Re-run: `export REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://ascent-windows.preview.emergentagent.com && pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Key data-testids for PayPal UI: invoice-paypal-{id}, paypal-webhook-url, integration-paypal. IMAP timeout added post-iter-5 did not break batch 3 (all 29 pass). Webhook signature + owner-scoped status are the only material follow-ups before production cutover.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no bugs. Initial test drafts assumed a single-invoice GET endpoint (405) and a hard stripe=connected assertion that fails when prior test runs toggled Stripe off; both tests were corrected to use the list endpoint and to assert presence instead of status. No backend code changes were required.
